design_philosophy.core_loop: Fast-paced 2-on-2 basketball with arcade action mechanics: score, get 'on fire' with three consecutive baskets, maintain hot streak momentum while opponent defense escalates
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Momentum swings between 'on fire' hot streaks (unlimited turbo, precision shooting) and cold streaks; aggressive offensive flow vs. defensive positioning without traditional fouls
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Superhuman basketball player making spectacular dunks defying physics and gravity; arcade spectacle over simulation realism
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate rejection of simulation realism in favor of exaggerated, over-the-top arcade action. Removes fouls and free throws entirely, enabling aggressive physical play. The 'on fire' mechanic creates volatile momentum-based gameplay rather than consistent skill expression. This was intentionally designed to appeal to broader audiences than traditional sports sims.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No documented difficulty settings or accessibility features. Gameplay difficulty emerges from opponent AI adaptation and momentum swings in arcade matches.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden players unlocked via button combinations and secret codes (e.g., Bill Clinton, Charlotte Hornets mascot Hugo, digitized amateur players). Tournament Edition implies tournament/ladder progression structure.
replayability_hooks: Extensive hidden characters and easter eggs (Super Clean Floors mode, tank mini-game)
Multiple secret player unlocks via cheat codes
Multiplayer competitive matches with team variation
Different rosters per console/arcade version encourage multiple playthroughs

world_building_techniques: NBA-licensed real teams and digitized likenesses of actual players grounds arcade fantasy in recognizable basketball culture
Cosmetic easter eggs (celebrity characters, mascots) build personality without explicit narrative

faction_indicators: NBA team selection creates sports identity signaling
Michael Jordan absence becomes notorious social talking point (licensing controversy)
environmental_storytelling: Digitized real player likenesses anchor fantasy dunks in authentic basketball visual language
NBA team rosters reflect real-world seasons (1992-93 arcade, 1993-94 console), grounding game in contemporary sports culture
